headline: Sierra Leone Program Helps Amputees Rebuild Lives Through Farming
meta_description: An innovative farming program in Sierra Leone teaches people with limb loss sustainable agriculture skills, enabling them to start their own businesses.
summary_short: A groundbreaking farming initiative in Sierra Leone empowers people with limb loss to build new lives through sustainable agriculture and entrepreneurship.
body:
In Sierra Leone, a transformative program is opening new pathways for people living with limb loss. The initiative teaches sustainable farming practices to amputees, equipping them with practical skills to launch their own agricultural businesses.

By combining vocational training with access to land and resources, the program addresses both economic and social needs. Participants learn not only how to grow crops sustainably but also how to manage and market their produce, creating viable livelihoods rather than dependency on aid.

The program recognizes that people with disabilities possess the same drive and capability as anyone else—they simply need the right tools and opportunity. For many participants, the chance to build independent income through farming has become a pathway to dignity, stability, and community reintegration. The model offers a powerful reminder that inclusion and practical support can turn challenges into opportunities.
